# iosxe_eigrp_vrf (Resource)

This resource can manage the EIGRP VRF configuration.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "iosxe_eigrp_vrf" "example" {
  name              = "TOPGEN"
  vrf               = "VRF1"
  autonomous_system = 100
  router_id         = "10.255.1.1"
  networks = [
    {
      ip       = "10.20.0.0"
      wildcard = "0.0.255.255"
    }
  ]
  auto_summary = false
  shutdown     = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `autonomous_system` (Number) Autonomous system number
  - Range: `1`-`65535`
- `name` (String) EIGRP virtual-instance name
- `vrf` (String) VRF name

### Optional

- `auto_summary` (Boolean) Enable automatic network number summarization
  - Default value: `false`
- `delete_mode` (String) Configure behavior when deleting/destroying the resource. Either delete the entire object (YANG container) being managed, or only delete the individual resource attributes configured explicitly and leave everything else as-is. Default value is `all`.
  - Choices: `all`, `attributes`
- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.
- `networks` (Attributes List) Enable routing on an IP network (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--networks))
- `router_id` (String) Router ID for this EIGRP process
- `shutdown` (Boolean) Shutdown address family
- `topology_base` (String) Base topology (always base)
  - Choices: `base`
  - Default value: `base`

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The path of the object.

<a id="nestedatt--networks"></a>
### Nested Schema for `networks`

Required:

- `ip` (String) Network number
- `wildcard` (String) EIGRP wild card bits

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import iosxe_eigrp_vrf.example "<name>,<vrf>"
```
